Question 6: HDFS Daemons:-
Daemon is a process that runs in the background. It is mainly done in the Java. There are various daemons present in Hadoop.
i) Name Node
ii) Data Node
iii) Secondary Name Node
iv) Resource Node
v)

What is Hadoop?
Hadoop is a free and open source software used for managing & mentoring of huge amount of data. It can handle structured, unstructured, semi-structured data. Hadoop uses the distributed manner to store the large amount of files. Then by Map Reduce it is parallelly processing the files & generating the output.

Feature of Hadoop:-
• Can handle large amount of data.
• Can visualize large amount of data.
• No need of SQL structures i.e No SQL.
• Columnar way of storing.

Hadoop Daemon:-
→ It schedules, manages the files in the background.
i) Name Node:-
Name node is mainly responsible to store the Metadata of file. It don't store the actual content of the file. It is the Master Node. It manages the file name, date of modification, last modified.

Question 6 (continued): in the form of Metadata.
Features of Name node:-
• It do not store the actual data of the file.
• It increases the memory speed & reduces RAM usage.

ii) Data Node:-
Data node is responsible to store the data in HDFS (Hadoop distributed file System). It manages the data present in the file. It is governed by the Name Node. i.e Name Node instruct the data Node when to store the data.

iii) Secondary Name Node:-
It tells the when the data is present. It is the global Master Node that controls the flow of the Data from Name Node to Data Node. It stores the all the log files in the file called 'fsimage'.

| Hadoop Daemon | Port Number |
|

Question 6 (continued): a) Resource Manager:-
as the name suggests it manages the all Resources present in the Hadoop ecosystem.

b) YARN (Yet another Resource Negotiator):-
since, in the Hadoop V1 the resource management & the data processing was the part of Map Reduce due to more amount of data we have to get lot of lag in the processing. as a result from next versions the YARN (Yet another Resource Negotiator) was introduced.

| Hadoop Daemon's | Port Number |
|

Question 8: Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS) architecture:-
Coming to Hadoop architecture it has got various parts such as Jobtracker, Data Node, Name Node, Tracker ID.
It is a Master & slave management, where the Master Node has Jobtracker, Data Node, Name Node, Tracker ID. It has various slaves working under the Master Node. Below diagram demonstrate the Hadoop distributed File System (HDFS) Architecture.

[Diagram: A flowchart/block diagram showing a 'Master Node' box containing 'Job Tracker', 'Tracking ID', 'Name Node', 'Data Node', connected to three 'Slaves' each containing 'Tracking ID' and 'Data Node']

MapReduce:- it is a Data Processing unit in the Hadoop ecosystem, which processes the distributed manner files in a parallel way & generates the output.

[Diagram: Block diagram showing Distributed files → MapReduce → Parallel Processing]

Question 8 (continued): Hadoop:- Hadoop is a open source platform that is used to manage the huge amount of data. more over, to be stored in the distributed manner.

JobTracker:- it is used to divide the large number of jobs into smaller jobs & assign it to the tracker id. so that Tracker can give to the respective slave to generate the output.

Name Node:- it is used to maintain the all task lives & its works that are given. It gulds the data node.

Data Node:- it handles the storing of the data in the HDFS & particular ID given to that slave, it helps in maintaining the data properly.

Question 10: Machine Learning Techniques:-
Machine learning is a term that trains a model to learn from the data given & then predict the future values based on the trained data. we have various types of Machine learning techniques.
i) Supervised Learning
ii) Unsupervised Learning
iii) Semi-supervised Learning
iv) Reinforcement Learning.

i) Supervised learning:-
a labelled data is given to the model training. label data means the X & Y coordinates, for example let us consider the 'House Price Prediction'. In this we are giving the labelled data as Price & area of the House in sq feet.
so, the price is the 'y' as it is the output. & the area is in the input therefore it is 'x'.

Techniques:-
i) Linear Regression
ii) K-mean Algorithm.
iii) Multi linear Regression.

[Graph: Line chart with 'Price' on Y-axis and 'Area' on X-axis, showing a linear trend labeled 'Linear Regression']

Question 10 (continued): ii) Unsupervised learning:-
We are not giving the labelled data for training. When we give the unlabelled data for training we are training the model using unsupervised learning techniques. for example:- let us consider the 'Recommendation System' where initially we don't have the data of user. So it doesn't show the recommendation.
Once, the user starts browsing the web, it keeps on tracing it which is unlabelled data & then recommendation comes based on the searching pattern.

[Diagram: Flowchart showing "cat, dog, cows, buffaloes" → "unsupervised learning" → "Model" → "cow"]
[Diagram: "cow" → Input → "Model" → "cow"]

iii) Reinforcement learning:-
Reinforcement learning means learning from mistake.
for example:- let us suppose we have a Robot, so, there are two moves 1) step to the right option 2) step to the fire place.
if the the Robot steps to the fire place he will get the negative positive rewarding. So next time Robot will remember not to step in fire. in the way of Rewards & penalty manner the training method is referred as Reinforcement learning.

Question 11: Social Media Analytics:-
Analysing the social trends such as most liked products, most commuted products, will helps the sales person to earn money. So, social media Analytics means analysing the social media produced data & getting a visualization to help the end user.

Feature/Advantages of Social Media Analytics:-
→ helps to learn the Market.
→ understand the human/end-user mentality.
→ helps to understand the future/present needs.
→ helps in generating revenue.
→ to understand the Demand & Supply

For example:-
let us take Amazon, it will use the Social Media Analysis in way that it will help the Amazon to learn the most buyed product, most replaced product. so, that Amazon can understand the demand of particular product.

Importance of Social Media Analytics:-
→ it helps the vendor to understand the demand of particular product & helps to earn the revenue.
→ helps in generating the revenue.
→ helps in visualizing the demand in easy way.
→ understand the present scenario.
→ If the vendor understand the social media Analytics he will be able to grow & learn from the market.

Question 11 (continued): [Graph: Line graph with 'demand' on Y-axis and 'price' on X-axis, showing an upward trend]

if the vendor understand the above graph he will get to know the effect of price on demand, as a result he will have a proper mindset of the people.

→ visualization helps in the understanding the data clearly.

Question 1: The input & outputs of Map Reduce:
The input of the MapReduce is the flume, sqoop collected & stored data. flume collects the unstructured data. & sqoop collects the structured data.
The output of MapReduce is the list, json form. which is given to the cloudera to plot or visualize the output of MapReduce.

Question 2: Data moved in & out of Hadoop:
The input of the hadoop is the three types of data i) structured ii) unstructured iii) semi-structured. these input is taken by the flume & sqoop, that is stored in HBase, HDFS.
The output is produce in the cloudera to the end user.

Question 3: Secondary Name Node:-
→ it main function of secondary Name Node is to generate/store the logs, if the system fails & then the user will get helped with the log generated every minute.
→ the log is stored in the file called 'fsimage'.
→ it is the global master node.
→ logs are generated every minute in order to avoid the crashes.

Question 4: Supervised learning:-
When/while training the data we are giving the labelled data this method of training is referred as supervised learning.
→ technique involves :-
i) linear regression
ii) classification
iii) Kmean algorithm.

[Graph: Line graph with 'price' on Y-axis and 'Square feet (area)' on X-axis, showing upward linear trend labeled 'linear Regression']
